revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:北辰妙見神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hokushin Myōken Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Hokusinmyoken-jinjya3.jpg | location = 946 Taki, Katsuragi Town, Ito District, Wakayama, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327444|N|135.496944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = Principal deity: Myōken-son
Sub-deities: Susanoo, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Miketsu no Mikoto, Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto | founded = Unknown | festival = April 23 | website = | prefecture= {{ill|Katsuragi|qid=Q1345594|1=Katsuragi, Wakayama}} }} '''Hokushin Myōken Shrine''' (北辰妙見神社, ''Hokushin Myōken Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Katsuragi|en|Katsuragi|qid=Q417423|lt=Katsuragi|1=Katsuragi}}, [[:en:Ito District|Ito District]], {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, Japan. The shrine enshrines {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken-son|1=Myōken}} as its principal deity. It is notable for its historic bell, which has been designated as a [[tangible cultural property]]. == History and Overview == The shrine’s exact founding date is unknown, but it is mentioned in the ''[[Kii Zoku Fudoki]]'' under the name "Myōken-sha" in the section on Takimura in [[Shigo Village]]. The shrine is associated with [[:en:Pole Star|Polaris]] and the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|qid=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper|1=Big Dipper}}, collectively referred to as ''Hokushin'' ("Northern Stars") and regarded as the central axis of heaven. These stars were deified as ''Myōken-son'' and are commonly referred to as "Myōken-sama." Myōken-son is a foundational deity in [[Onmyōdō]] and {{ill|Feng shui|en|Feng shui|qid=Q170537|lt=Feng shui|1=Feng shui}}. The original shrine building was constructed in the ''Gongen-zukuri'' architectural style but was rebuilt in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} in the ''Nagare-zukuri'' style with vibrant coloration. Regular renovations occur every 20 years, with the most recent in 1994, during which the shrine’s colors were restored and the worship hall rebuilt. The Taki district has long been a hub of fervent religious devotion. Nearby are five temples and sacred halls. In addition to the shrine's own cultural assets, the area features significant artifacts, such as the [[:en:National Treasure|National Treasure]] bronze "Half-Seated Maitreya Bodhisattva" statue (from the {{ill|Hakuhō period|en|Hakuhō period|qid=Q373860|lt=Hakuhō period|1=Hakuhō period}}) at Gokuraku-ji Temple and a prefectural cultural property, the ''Daihannya Kyō'' (Great Wisdom Sutra), consisting of 569 scrolls dating back to {{ill|1227|en|1227|qid=Q5407|lt=1227|1=1227}} (''Karoku'' 3).{{Cite web|url=https://www.katsuragi-kanko.jp/search/syazi/%e5%8c%97%e8%be%b0%e5%a6%99%e8%a6%8b%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|title=北辰妙見神社|publisher=Katsuragi Town Tourism Association|accessdate=2020-11-03}}{{Cite web|url=http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4017|title=北辰妙見神社|publisher=Wakayama Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-11-03}} == Enshrined Deities == * '''Principal deity:''' {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken-son|1=Myōken}} * '''Sub-Deities:''' ** [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]] ** {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (linked to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) ** [[Miketsu no Mikoto]] (one of the [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]) ** [[Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto]] (a goddess of the sea and one of the [[Munakata Sanjin]]) == Cultural Assets and Grounds == === Bell Tower === * The shrine’s bronze bell dates to ''Bunei'' 2 (1265) and was designated a {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} tangible cultural property on April 14, 1965.{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.katsuragi.wakayama.jp/020/070/20170406185058.html|title=指定文化財一覧（県指定文化財）|publisher=Katsuragi Town|accessdate=2020-11-03}} === Shrine Buildings === The grounds include: * '''Main Hall''' (''Nagare-zukuri'' style with copper roofing) * '''Ebisu Shrine''' (''Nagare-zukuri'' style) * '''Worship Hall''' (wooden, tiled roof) * '''Star Service Hall''' (''Hoshi Kuyo-do'', wooden, tiled roof) * '''Shrine Office''' (wooden, tiled roof) Hokusinmyoken-jinjya Syoro.jpg|Bell Tower Hokushinmyoken-jinjya Honden.jpg|Main Hall == Local Information == === Address === 946 Taki, Katsuragi Town, Ito District, Wakayama, Japan === Access === * '''By Car:''' 20 minutes from [[Kasada Station]] (JR {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|qid=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}}). * 15 minutes from [[Katsuragi Nishi Interchange]] (''Kyōnan-Wa Expressway''). == Toyohara Family Residence == [[File:Toyohara House.jpg|thumb|Toyohara Family Residence]] Located in the Taki district, the Toyohara Family Residence has been designated a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] since December 5, 2019.{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.katsuragi.wakayama.jp/20191210134032.html|title=豊原家住宅・的場家住宅・神野阿弥陀堂が登録有形文化財に登録されました！|publisher=Katsuragi Town|accessdate=2021-02-07}} The residence comprises: * '''Main House:''' Constructed during the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, renovated in 2019. A single-story wooden structure with a thatched roof and ''irimoya'' style, covering 102 square meters. * '''Detached Sitting Room:''' Built in the early {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}, it features an ''irimoya'' roof, tile roofing, and measures 64 square meters. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[List of Important Cultural Properties in Kinki]] * [[List of Wakayama Prefecture Cultural Properties]] * [[List of Registered Tangible Cultural Properties]] == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:Hokushin-myoken-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=4017 Hokushin Myōken Shrine] – Wakayama Shrine Office * {{Cultural Heritage Online|403178|Toyohara Family Main Residence}} * {{Cultural Heritage Online|458409|Toyohara Family Detached Sitting Room}} __NOINDEX__ ==References== {{reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{translated page|ja|北辰妙見神社|version=97793220|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[de:Yatsushiro Shrine]] [[ja:北辰妙見神社]] {{Commons category|Hokushin-myoken-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q106852427}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Wakayama]] [[Category:Katsuragi, Wakayama]] [[Category:Municipal History of Katsuragi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wooden buildings and structures in Japan]]
